687:## PreRun and PostRun Hooks
689:It is possible to run functions before or after the main `Run` function of your command. The `PersistentPreRun` and `PreRun` functions will be executed before `Run`. `PersistentPostRun` and `PostRun` will be executed after `Run`.  The `Persistent*Run` functions will be inherited by children if they do not declare their own.  The `*PreRun` and `*PostRun` functions will only be executed if the `Run` function of the current command has been declared.  These functions are run in the following order:
691:- `PersistentPreRun`
692:- `PreRun`
694:- `PostRun`
695:- `PersistentPostRun`
697:An example of two commands which use all of these features is below.  When the subcommand is executed, it will run the root command's `PersistentPreRun` but not the root command's `PersistentPostRun`:
713:    PersistentPreRun: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
714:      fmt.Printf("Inside rootCmd PersistentPreRun with args: %v\n", args)
716:    PreRun: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
717:      fmt.Printf("Inside rootCmd PreRun with args: %v\n", args)
722:    PostRun: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
723:      fmt.Printf("Inside rootCmd PostRun with args: %v\n", args)
725:    PersistentPostRun: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
726:      fmt.Printf("Inside rootCmd PersistentPostRun with args: %v\n", args)
733:    PreRun: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {

739:    PostRun: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {

742:    PersistentPostRun: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {

218	### Returning and handling errors
   219	
   220	If you wish to return an error to the caller of a command, `RunE` can be used.
   221	
   222	```go
   223	package cmd
   224	
   225	import (
   226	  "fmt"
   227	
   228	  "github.com/spf13/cobra"
   229	)
   230	
   231	func init() {
   232	  rootCmd.AddCommand(tryCmd)
   233	}
   234	
   235	var tryCmd = &cobra.Command{
   236	  Use:   "try",
   237	  Short: "Try and possibly fail at something",
   238	  RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
   239	    if err := someFunc(); err != nil {
   240		return err
   241	    }
   242	    return nil
   243	  },
   244	}
   245	```
   246	
   247	The error can then be caught at the execute function call.
   248	
   249	## Working with Flags
   250	
   251	Flags provide modifiers to control how the action command operates.
   252
